text.title: QA Flake Ledger Template Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-flake-ledger-template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Each flaky test has exactly one row keyed by full test id (path::test_name). Duplicate rows are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Per-row ownership makes burn-down measurable; multi-row scatter makes it impossible.

rule.statement: Each row carries a single owner handle (no team). Owner is assigned from CODEOWNERS on first ingest.
rule.rationale: Without a name, flakes age indefinitely.

rule.statement: fail_count, run_count, first_seen, last_seen are populated from CI history, not manual entry.
rule.rationale: Manual entries drift; only CI numbers are auditable.

rule.statement: Each row carries a root_cause from the flaky-test-root-cause-taxonomy enum, set within 7 days of first seen.
rule.rationale: Untyped flakes get re-investigated repeatedly; taxonomy tags route the fix.

rule.statement: Tests with fail_rate > 5% over ≥10 runs in the last 7d auto-promote to quarantine state.
rule.rationale: Threshold-driven quarantine prevents heroic 'one more retry' attempts that block CI.

rule.statement: Ledger is reviewed weekly. Rows with state=quarantine + age > 30d trigger a fix-or-delete decision.
rule.rationale: Quarantine without review is a graveyard; review forces decision.

rule.statement: If the test suite is < 50 tests OR sustained flake rate is < 1% OR policy explicitly forbids quarantine, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Below threshold the ledger is more bookkeeping than benefit.
